codeReview.summary: The fix does address the original scrutiny finding by starting the new file uploads concurrently and preserving merged file-id order before batch creation, but it is not safe to ship as written because the new worker-thread fan-out returns on the first upload error without joining the remaining workers, so the helper can report failure while detached upload threads continue mutating `/v1/files` in the background.

issues.description: `upload_and_poll` now spawns one thread per file, but the `for worker in upload_workers` loop uses `worker.join()??`, so the helper returns immediately on the first upload error or panic. Any later handles in `upload_workers` are then dropped without being joined, which leaves those upload threads running after the method has already failed. A caller can therefore receive an error while other `/v1/files` uploads are still completing in the background and creating orphaned files, which breaks the synchronous helper contract and makes error handling nondeterministic.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared mock HTTP harness only supports serialized request/response scripts, so concurrency-sensitive transport proofs currently require ad hoc loopback servers inside individual tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `tests/support/mock_http.rs:62-90` accepts and serves one connection per scripted response in sequence, while this fix had to add a bespoke `ConcurrentUploadServer` at `tests/vector_store_file_batches_contract.rs:444-583` to prove overlapping uploads.

summary: Reviewed the fix handoff, transcript skeleton, prior failed review, feature definitions, and both commit diffs (`241729dfe56200f6fb866a6be38bd5c8f6bc3587` and `5bf718210784bb2f117c34847271d96c6408172d`). The concurrency regression is fixed in the success path, but `src/resources/vector_stores.rs:579-603` introduces a new failure-path bug: the helper exits on the first failed worker without joining the remaining upload threads, so background `/v1/files` mutations can continue after the call has already returned an error.
